one_line_pitch: A strategy-defined vectorization and pattern-matching trading system that decides LONG/SHORT/PASS based on historical pattern statistics within the same strategy, version, and symbol.
problem: Discretionary trading lacks consistency because decisions rely on intuition rather than statistical evidence from similar historical patterns. This system eliminates guesswork by vectorizing strategy-defined events and using L2 similarity search against past patterns to make evidence-based entry decisions.
target_users: Single private crypto futures trader developing and running quantitative strategies

primary_stack.strategy_sandbox: TypeScript runtime sandbox (DB-stored, Pine Script-level API)

core_capabilities: Dynamic strategy system: TypeScript strategies stored in DB, executed in sandbox with Pine Script-level API (candle data, technical indicators, timeframe access, OHLCV logic)
Mandatory vector definition: strategies must define features[] with normalization methods, all features normalized to [0,1]
Event vectorization pipeline: candle close → strategy condition → event → vectorize → L2 similarity search → statistics → decision → alert/trade
Statistical decision engine: winrate ≥ 55%, expectancy > 0, min_samples ≥ 30 required for entry
Result labeling: TP/SL/TIME_EXIT judgment with pnl_pct, mfe_pct, mae_pct, hold_bars tracking
Backtesting: 3-year historical data validation with vector generation
Four execution modes: Analysis → Alert → Paper trading → Auto-trading (all in MVP)
Strict isolation: vector search scoped to same strategy + version + symbol only
Strategy versioning: new version = new vector table + historical data re-vectorization
constraints: Candle close → alert/trade latency: under 1 second
Architecture must have no ceiling on concurrent strategies/symbols
24/7 uptime target (initial deployment: local laptop, single user)
Vector isolation: no cross-strategy or cross-symbol vector comparison
New strategy version requires new physical vector table + re-vectorization
Strategy code must be DB-stored and sandbox-executed at runtime

quality_attributes.performance: < 1s candle-to-decision latency; backtest 3yr in minutes; pgvector HNSW for sub-100ms vector search
quality_attributes.reliability: 24/7 operation with graceful recovery; candle continuity validation; idempotent event processing
quality_attributes.security: Strategy sandbox isolation; exchange API key encryption; no external code execution outside sandbox
quality_attributes.developer_experience: Bun-native tooling; type-safe DrizzleORM schemas; hot-reload dev server; comprehensive test coverage for strategy API

repo_commands.backtest: bun run backtest  [defined in EP05; not available until EP05 is complete]

assumptions: PostgreSQL with pgvector extension is available
Exchange API keys are pre-configured by the operator
CCXT library handles exchange-specific protocol differences
Strategy sandbox isolation mechanism TBD (Bun worker threads vs V8 isolates — validated in EP02-M0 PoC)
Initial deployment is local laptop; cloud migration is a future concern
Tauri and Next.js share a common API layer but have separate UI codebases
